overview: The Regex Filter extracts translatable text from any text-based format using user-defined regular expressions with capturing groups. Each rule pairs a regex pattern with an action (extract content, extract strings, treat as comment, start/end section, or skip), and capturing groups map matched text to source, target, identifier, and note roles. The filter processes rules in order, scanning the document from top to bottom and applying the first matching rule at each position.

preserveWhiteSpaces.help: Controls whitespace handling in extracted text. When disabled, consecutive whitespace sequences (spaces, tabs, CR, LF) are collapsed to a single space, and leading/trailing whitespace is trimmed.

hasInlineCodes.help: Enables conversion of parts of extracted text into inline codes. When enabled, use the inline codes patterns editor to define which text patterns become codes.

autoCloseSection.help:
Automatically sends an `END_GROUP` event before each new `START_GROUP`. This means you only need **Start a section** rules — no matching **End a section** rules are required.

If disabled, every **Start a section** rule **must** have a corresponding **End a section** rule.
notes: When enabled, you **must not** define explicit End a section rules.

dotAll.help:
Makes the `.` operator match line-feed characters in addition to all other characters. Applies to all rules globally.

To override per-rule, use the `(?s)` flag construct in that rule's pattern.

multiLine.help:
Makes `^` and `$` match at line terminators within the input, not just at the start and end of the entire input sequence. Applies to all rules globally.

To override per-rule, use the `(?m)` flag construct in that rule's pattern.

ignoreCase.help:
Makes pattern matching case-insensitive. When enabled, `abc` matches `Abc`, `ABC`, etc. Applies to all rules globally.

To override per-rule, use the `(?i)` flag construct in that rule's pattern.

useLocDir.help: Enables recognition of localization directives embedded in the input file. When disabled, all localization directives are ignored.

extractOutsideLocDir.help: Controls whether translatable items outside the scope of any localization directive are extracted. Useful for minimizing markup — you can annotate only the exceptions rather than every translatable segment.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this option to take effect

startString.help:
Defines the character(s) that mark the beginning of a string, used by the **Extract the strings in the source group** action.

Multiple characters define multiple alternative string delimiters. Each character position must correspond to its matching end character.
startString.values: One or more single characters. E.g., `"` or `"'` (double-quote and single-quote as two alternatives).
examples: `"'` — strings delimited by either `"` or `'` (pair with `"'` in End of string)

endString.help: Defines the character(s) that mark the end of a string. Must have the same number of characters as **Beginning of string**, with each position corresponding to its matching start character.
endString.values: One or more single characters, positionally paired with start characters.

dependsOn.condition: must have equal number of characters, positionally paired

useBackSlashEscape.help: Characters escaped with a backslash prefix (e.g., `\"`) are treated as literal content, not string delimiters.

dependsOn.condition: conflicts — only one escape mechanism should be active

useDoubleCharEscape.help: Characters escaped by doubling (e.g., `""`) are treated as literal content, not string delimiters.

dependsOn.condition: conflicts — only one escape mechanism should be active

mimeType.help: The MIME type identifies the document format and may affect how translated text is written back. For most use cases, `text/plain` is appropriate.

rules.help:
Ordered list of extraction rules. Each rule has a Java regular expression, an action, and capturing group assignments for source, target, identifier, and note.

Rules are evaluated **in list order** — the first matching rule at each position wins. Order matters for correct extraction.

**Capturing groups** are numbered by opening parenthesis from left to right (group 0 = entire match). Assign groups to roles:
- **Source group** — the translatable text (required for extract actions)
- **Target group** — pre-existing translation (use `-1` if undefined)
- **Identifier group** — resource name/ID (use `-1` if undefined)
- **Note group** — translator note (use `-1` if undefined)

**Actions:**
- **Extract the strings in the source group** — finds delimited strings within the source group (delimiters set in Options/Strings)
- **Extract the content of the source group** — extracts the entire source group as one text unit
- **Treat as comment** — scans for localization directives but does not extract
- **Do not extract** — skips the match
- **Start a section** / **End a section** — creates `START_GROUP`/`END_GROUP` events
examples: `^\[(.*?)\](=|:)(.*?)$` with source=3, id=1 — extracts `key=value` or `key:value` pairs

limitations: The **entire document is loaded into memory** to apply regular expressions — very large documents may cause memory issues.
The **Extract strings outside the rules** option is not yet implemented.
The filter does not recognize encoding declarations in the document and therefore cannot update them automatically.
processingNotes: **Encoding detection**: Unicode BOM is detected and used automatically; otherwise falls back to the default encoding specified when opening the document.
**BOM handling**: UTF-8 output includes a BOM only if the input was also UTF-8 and had a BOM. Transcoding from non-UTF-8 to UTF-8 never adds a BOM.
**Line-breaks**: Output preserves the same line-break style as the input.
**Parsing order**: Rules are evaluated in list order at each position; the first match wins and the search position advances to the end of that match.
**Regex options**: `dotAll`, `multiLine`, and `ignoreCase` apply globally to all rules. Per-rule overrides are possible using the `(?idmsux-idmsux)` inline flag construct within the pattern.
**Metadata rules**: FPRM-only `metaRule` parameters allow adding matched regex content as metadata on TextUnits (not accessible via UI).

examples.title: Key-value extraction
examples.description: Extract translatable values from a simple `key=value` or `key:value` format, using the key as the resource identifier.
examples.config:
```
Rule expression: ^\[(.*?)\](=|:)(.*?)$
Action: Extract the content of the source group
Source group: 3
Identifier group: 1
Regex options: Multi-line enabled
```
examples.output:
```xml
<trans-unit id="1" resname="ID1" xml:space="preserve">
  <source xml:lang="en">Text for ID1</source>
</trans-unit>
<trans-unit id="2" resname="ID2" xml:space="preserve">
  <source xml:lang="en">Text for ID2</source>
</trans-unit>
```

examples.title: FPRM metadata rules
examples.description: Add regex-matched content as metadata on TextUnits. This is configured only via FPRM parameters, not the UI.

preserveWhiteSpaces.title: Preserve White Spaces
preserveWhiteSpaces.description: Preserve all white spaces in extracted text instead of collapsing and trimming them.

hasInlineCodes.title: Has Inline Codes
hasInlineCodes.description: Enable conversion of text patterns into inline codes.

autoCloseSection.title: Auto-Close Sections
autoCloseSection.description: Automatically close the previous section when a new one starts.

dotAll.title: Dot Matches Line-Feed
dotAll.description: Enable the dot operator to match line-feed characters.

multiLine.title: Multi-Line Mode
multiLine.description: Make ^ and $ match at line terminators, not just start and end of input.

ignoreCase.title: Ignore Case
ignoreCase.description: Ignore letter case differences when matching patterns.

useLocDir.title: Use Localization Directives
useLocDir.description: Recognize and process localization directives in the input.

extractOutsideLocDir.title: Extract Outside Directives
extractOutsideLocDir.description: Extract translatable items that are not within the scope of a localization directive.

startString.title: Beginning of String
startString.description: Character(s) marking the start of a string for string extraction.

endString.title: End of String
endString.description: Character(s) marking the end of a string for string extraction.

useBackSlashEscape.title: Backslash Escape
useBackSlashEscape.description: Use backslash prefix to escape characters in strings.

useDoubleCharEscape.title: Double Character Escape
useDoubleCharEscape.description: Use character doubling to escape characters in strings.

mimeType.title: Document MIME Type
mimeType.description: MIME type used to identify the document format during extraction.

rules.title: Extraction Rules
rules.description: Ordered list of regex-based rules with actions and capturing group assignments.

fullDoc:
# Regex Filter

The Regex Filter extracts translatable text from any text-based format using user-defined regular expressions with capturing groups. Each rule pairs a regex pattern with an action (extract content, extract strings, treat as comment, start/end section, or skip), and capturing groups map matched text to source, target, identifier, and note roles. The filter processes rules in order, scanning the document from top to bottom and applying the first matching rule at each position.

## Parameters

### Rules

#### Rules
Ordered list of extraction rules. Each rule has a Java regular expression, an action, and capturing group assignments for source, target, identifier, and note.

Rules are evaluated **in list order** — the first matching rule at each position wins. Order matters for correct extraction.

**Capturing groups** are numbered by opening parenthesis from left to right (group 0 = entire match). Assign groups to roles:
- **Source group** — the translatable text (required for extract actions)
- **Target group** — pre-existing translation (use `-1` if undefined)
- **Identifier group** — resource name/ID (use `-1` if undefined)
- **Note group** — translator note (use `-1` if undefined)

**Actions:**

| Action | Effect | Source | Target | Identifier | Note |
|--------|--------|--------|--------|------------|------|
| Extract the strings in source group | `TEXT_UNIT` per string found | Required — contains strings | Not used | Name for first TU (sequential suffix for others) | Note for each TU |
| Extract the content of source group | Single `TEXT_UNIT` | Required — source text | Target text | TU name | TU note |
| Treat as comment | Scan for loc directives | Required — scanned | Not used | Not used | Not used |
| Do not extract | Skip | Not used | Not used | Not used | Not used |
| Start a section | `START_GROUP` event | Not used | Not used | Section name | Section note |
| End a section | `END_GROUP` event | Not used | Not used | Not used | Not used |

#### Preserve White Spaces
Preserve all white spaces in extracted text. When disabled, consecutive whitespace (spaces, tabs, CR, LF) is collapsed to a single space and leading/trailing whitespace is trimmed.

#### Has Inline Codes
Enable conversion of text patterns into inline codes. Configure patterns via the Inline Codes Patterns editor.

#### Auto-Close Sections
Automatically close the previous section when a new one starts. When enabled, only define **Start a section** rules — do **not** define matching **End a section** rules. When disabled, every start must have a corresponding end.

### Regular Expression Options

These options apply globally to all rules. Override per-rule using `(?idmsux-idmsux)` inline flag syntax in the pattern.

#### Dot Matches Line-Feed
Makes the `.` operator match line-feed characters (equivalent to Java `Pattern.DOTALL`).

#### Multi-Line Mode
Makes `^` and `$` match at line terminators within the input, not just at the absolute start/end (equivalent to Java `Pattern.MULTILINE`).

#### Ignore Case
Makes matching case-insensitive — `abc` matches `Abc`, `ABC`, etc. (equivalent to Java `Pattern.CASE_INSENSITIVE`).

### Localization Directives

#### Use Localization Directives
Enables the filter to recognize localization directives. When disabled, all directives are ignored.

#### Extract Outside Directives
Extract translatable items outside the scope of localization directives. Only active when **Use Localization Directives** is enabled.

### Strings

These settings control the **Extract the strings in the source group** action.

#### Beginning of String
Character(s) marking the start of a string. Multiple characters define multiple alternative delimiters, positionally paired with end characters.

#### End of String
Character(s) marking the end of a string. Must have the same count as beginning characters, with each position corresponding to its pair.

#### Backslash Escape
Treat backslash-prefixed characters (e.g., `\"`) as escaped literals, not delimiters.

#### Double Character Escape
Treat doubled characters (e.g., `""`) as escaped literals, not delimiters.

### Content Type

#### Document MIME Type
The MIME type for the document. Affects how text is written back. Use `text/plain` for most cases.

## Limitations

- The **entire document is loaded into memory** to apply regular expressions — very large documents may cause memory issues.
- The **Extract strings outside the rules** option is not yet implemented.
- The filter does not recognize encoding declarations in the document and therefore cannot update them automatically.

## Notes

- Unicode BOM is auto-detected; otherwise the default encoding specified when opening the document is used.
- UTF-8 output includes a BOM only if the input was also UTF-8 and had a BOM.
- Output preserves the input's line-break style.
- FPRM-only `metaRule` parameters allow adding matched regex content as metadata on TextUnits (not accessible via UI).

## Examples

### Key-Value Extraction

Extract translatable values from `key=value` or `key:value` format:

**Input:**
```
[ID1]=Text for ID1
[ID2]:Text for ID2
```

**Rule:** `^\[(.*?)\](=|:)(.*?)$` with source group = 3, identifier group = 1, multi-line enabled.

**Output (XLIFF):**
```xml
<trans-unit id="1" resname="ID1" xml:space="preserve">
  <source xml:lang="en">Text for ID1</source>
</trans-unit>
<trans-unit id="2" resname="ID2" xml:space="preserve">
  <source xml:lang="en">Text for ID2</source>
</trans-unit>
```

### FPRM Metadata Rules

Add regex-matched content as metadata (FPRM only, not in UI):

```
metaRuleCount.i=2
metaRule0.ruleName=meta1
metaRule0.expr=(\d\d:\d\d:\d\d)
metaRule1.ruleName=meta2
metaRule1.expr=(\d\d:\d\d:\d\d)
```
